items.title: Sustaining success across generations
items.desc: Family-owned businesses have long been the backbone of Thailand's economy, quietly powering growth, creating jobs and building wealth across generations. Yet beneath this strength lies a fragile reality, as most family enterprises struggle to survive beyond their founders.

items.title: BBL recommends Thai businesses mull expansion to India
items.desc: Bangkok Bank (BBL) is encouraging Thai businesses to expand into India to reduce their reliance on the US market amid rising geopolitical risk and escalating global trade tensions.

items.title: Pet products remain on clear growth trajectory
items.desc: The local pet industry is expected to remain resilient over the long term, as many owners treat their pets as family members. Over the past decade, the sector has recorded strong growth.

items.title: Dining sector poised for downturn
items.desc: Restaurants are bracing for less vibrant sales during the Songkran holiday this year, which runs from April 11-15, according to the Thai Restaurant Association.

items.title: Toyota to chat with authorities on oil, power
items.desc: Toyota Motor Thailand is preparing to hold talks with the new government on oil and electricity policies, as surging energy prices weigh heavily on consumers considering car purchases.

items.title: Global tensions hit home
items.desc: With the US-Israel battle against Iran entering its sixth week, the global economy continues to feel the effects stemming from the closure of a key oil shipping route, the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: 36 migrants found in hiding
items.desc: KANCHANABURI: Police arrested 36 individuals, including a local man, for harbouring undocumented foreign workers.

items.title: Russian tour guide arrested
items.desc: PHUKET: Tourist police arrested a Russian national for illegally offering tour services on a Destination Thailand Visa (DTV).

items.title: Tourists 'must be insured' before entering
items.desc: Thailand is pushing for regulations that would require international tourists to obtain accident insurance before entering the country, as rising unpaid medical bills and accident risks place a mounting strain on public healthcare.

items.title: Rajavithi completes first robotic heart operation
items.desc: Rajavithi Hospital has completed the country's first robotic heart surgery, marking a major advance in minimally invasive cardiac care.

items.title: Contested Clean Air Bill 'still worth a go'
items.desc: Severe haze in northern Thailand has intensified pressure on the government to adopt stronger legal tools, including advancing the contested Clean Air Bill.

items.title: Oman envoy talks Mideast  woes with tourist police
items.desc: Tourist Police and the Omani Embassy have held talks aimed at strengthening tourist safety, deepening bilateral cooperation and addressing the impact of Middle East tensions on travel confidence and regional transport.

items.title: Hoteliers call for more direct flights
items.desc: The Thai Hotels Association's Southern Chapter has urged the authorities to increase direct flights from Europe to Phuket to stabilise tourism amid travel disruptions caused by the Middle East war.

items.title: Anutin plans to present new measures to boost economy
items.desc: The government is preparing urgent economic and policy measures ahead of its parliamentary statement, aiming to boost growth, ease living costs and steer Thailand out of the middle income trap.

items.title: Extreme heat to scorch Northern, Central regions
items.desc: Temperatures could hit 42C this week as the Meteorological Department warns of scattered thunderstorms and gusty winds.

items.title: Oil depot reported for hoarding
items.desc: SURAT THANI: The provincial commerce office has filed a police complaint against an oil depot operator for allegedly hoarding controlled fuel, as authorities ramp up the crackdown on fuel stockpiling and smuggling in the South and along border areas.

items.title: No Brand brings standalone  K-grocery to Central Bangna
items.desc: No Brand, the renowned South Korean grocery store, recently launched its first standalone store in Thailand at Central Bangna, bringing thousands of products and introducing K-value retail experience for smart shoppers.

items.title: Asia Pillars offers 'Open Doors' C-suite networking at Park Hyatt Bangkok
items.desc: Asia Pillars is holding "Open Doors", an exclusive business networking event for non-members to connect and engage with influential business leaders, at Penthouse Bar+Grill of Park Hyatt Bangkok, Witthayu Road, on April 21 at 6pm.

items.title: Artemis astronauts begin fifth day on historic Moon mission
items.desc: HOUSTON - The four astronauts on NASA's Artemis 2 mission began their fifth day journeying to the Moon on Sunday, after already taking in sights of the lunar surface never before seen by human eyes.

items.title: Diesel surges past B50/litre
items.desc: Diesel prices have surged past 50 baht per litre, pushing up costs across the transport, tourism and agriculture sectors and prompting many Thais to either cancel or scale back their Songkran travel plans.

items.title: Navy disciplines officer for assaulting conscript
items.desc: The Royal Thai Navy has ordered the disciplinary detention of a commander for seven days after an investigation found he used excessive force against a naval conscript, in violation of navy regulations and standing orders.

items.title: Senator to grill health agency over hospital debt
items.desc: The chair of the Senate health committee plans to scrutinise the finances of the National Health Security Office (NHSO) during debate on the government's policy statement to parliament on April 9-10.

items.title: Hazardous levels of smog over most of Thailand
items.desc: Hazardous levels of ultrafine dust shrouded Bangkok and 58 of the 76 provinces late Sunday afternoon with red levels of smog in 10 northern and northeastern provinces, according to the Geo-Informatics and Space Technology Development Agency (Gistda).

items.title: Google Drive adds AI shield to crush ransomware threats
items.desc: Google has introduced a new ransomware detection and file recovery feature for Google Drive, aiming to strengthen data security as more users increasingly rely on cloud storage for important files.

items.title: 中国KOL扎堆打卡是隆！曼谷宋干节今年要这样玩
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月5日，曼谷市挽叻区办事处联合挽叻区发展参与推动委员会，以及多家政府与民间合作单位，共同启动“Songkran Splash Out with Thai Culture 2026”宣传预热活动，提前为今年备受关注的“是隆宋干节”造势升温。此次活动不仅延续了是隆路一贯的节庆热度，更将“传统泰式文化元素”与“国际游客传播”结合，释放出今年宋干节更鲜明的文化传播信号。 本次预热活动的一大亮点，是多位中国KOL及网络达人身穿泰式传统服饰、披上泰式披肩（Sabai）参与拍摄与宣传，以更贴近中国游客审美与社交媒体传播方式的内容表达，向外界传递今年是隆宋干节“玩水不止于玩水，文化体验同样值得打卡”的节庆新印象。 在以往印象中，宋干节往往意味着“水枪大战”“街头狂欢”和“游客集结”，但今年是隆路的活动传播方向，显然更进一步。官方正尝试通过“泰服穿搭+街区场景+节庆互动”的组合，把是隆从单一的“泼水地标”，升级为一个兼具视觉传播力、文化体验感与社交媒体分享属性的节日打卡地。 尤其值得关注的是，此次宣传中大量融入面向中国游客的传播语言与内容形式，释放出清晰信号：今年宋干节，曼谷不只是欢迎游客“来玩水”，更希望游客“玩得有内容、拍得有记忆、发得有话题”。 而“穿泰服去是隆”，也有望成为今年宋干节期间最具辨识度的新玩法之一。 作为曼谷每年宋干节期间最受关注的核心区域之一，是隆路一直是国

items.title: Security guard killed in Pattaya crash with Japanese driver
items.desc: Security guard killed in Pattaya crash with Japanese driver Legacy A security guard died after his motorcycle crashed into an SUV driven by a Japanese restaurant owner who was turning onto a main road in Pattaya early today, April 5. Police, rescue workers and a medical team were called to Phatthanakan Road in Nong Prue subdistrict, Bang Lamung district, Chon Buri at 3.49am after reports of a serious motorcycle crash . Debris was scattered across the road when they arrived. Photo via Siam Chon News A blue Honda motorcycle was found overturned with its front end heavily damaged. The rider, 27 y

items.title: Vendor splashed with hot oil by another worker in Krabi mall
items.desc: Vendor splashed with hot oil by another worker in Krabi mall Legacy A drink vendor was injured yesterday, April 4, after another worker threw hot cooking oil at her in a shopping mall in Mueang district, Krabi, with police detaining the suspect at the scene . Rescue workers were alerted by mall staff and coordinated with police before arriving at the food court on the first floor, where they found the victim, 19 year old Rattika, waiting for assistance. She had suffered burns to the right side of her torso and back after being splashed with hot oil used for frying food. First aid was given bef

items.title: 泰国暂停原棕榈油出口一年
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月5日，泰国政府通过政宪公报网站发布公告，宣布自2026年4月7日起，禁止向境外出口原棕榈油，为期一年，以稳定国内市场供应与价格。 该措施由泰国商品与服务价格委员会依据相关法律出台，适用于全国范围。公告明确指出，除获得官方许可外，任何企业与个人不得出口原棕榈油。 政府表示，受中东局势影响，国际油价持续上涨，能源领域对生物柴油的需求随之增加。同时，原棕榈油出口需求增加，进一步推高国内消耗。在内外需求同步增长的情况下，政府决定通过出口管制进行调节，以在消费、工业和能源等领域之间实现供需平衡，确保国内市场全年供应稳定。 根据规定，如确需出口，企业须向相关主管部门申请许可，并严格按照批准的种类、数量、时间及地点执行。每份许可证仅限单次使用，违规出口将被视为未获授权。 （编译：jinda；审校：wan；来源：matichon） The post 泰国暂停原棕榈油出口一年 appeared first on 泰国头条新闻 .

items.title: Cambodian worker dies fixing roof at Sri Racha factory
items.desc: Cambodian worker dies fixing roof at Sri Racha factory Legacy A 38 year old Cambodian man died after falling from a roof he was repairing at a wooden furniture factory in Sri Racha district, Chon Buri , yesterday afternoon. Rescue teams from Sawang Prateep Si Racha, together with Police Lieutenant Colonel Dechanat Thansirichuti, an investigator from Sri Racha Police Station, responded to the scene at a factory along Sukhumvit Road in Bang Phra subdistrict. They found the victim, Hao Hat, a 38 year old Cambodian national, on the factory floor, with his shoes scattered some distance from his bod

items.title: Trailer truck ploughs into traffic, injures 16 in Chachoengsao
items.desc: Trailer truck ploughs into traffic, injures 16 in Chachoengsao Legacy A 22-wheel trailer truck crashed into several vehicles on the Burapha Withi expressway in Bang Samak subdistrict, Bang Pakong district, Chachoengsao, at 4.30pm yesterday, April 4, leaving 16 people injured and blocking all lanes. Police, rescue workers and emergency responders went to kilometre marker 37 on the Chon Buri-bound side of the expressway, where they found the white trailer truck had collided with a white four-wheel lorry. The impact left the smaller truck badly damaged and threw its driver, 55 year old Chalee, fr

items.title: Australian climbs Pattaya balcony after dispute with Thai partner
items.desc: Australian climbs Pattaya balcony after dispute with Thai partner Legacy An Australian man was rescued after hanging from a third-floor hotel balcony in Pattaya , yesterday, April 4, after a domestic dispute led to what police described as another apparent bid for attention . Police and tourist officers responded to reports that a foreign man was threatening to jump from a hotel in Soi Buakhao. They found the 59 year old Australian hanging outside a third-floor balcony of the four-storey building. Hotel staff and police attempted to persuade him to return inside. He initially appeared to coope

items.title: Gang poses as officers to steal motorcycle in Pattaya raid
items.desc: Gang poses as officers to steal motorcycle in Pattaya raid Legacy Seven individuals allegedly impersonating narcotics officers stormed a home in Pattaya late on April 4, threatening a couple at gunpoint and knife-point before making off with a motorcycle. The incident took place at 10.46pm in the Nong Prue subdistrict of Pattaya . The group of seven claimed to be officers from the Office of the Narcotics Control Board (ONCB) as they raided the property. They left with a green Yamaha Forza motorcycle bearing a Chon Buri registration plate numbered 5 กค 4177. The victims, 30 year old tattoo arti

items.title: Trat farm worker killed by wild elephant in musth
items.desc: Trat farm worker killed by wild elephant in musth Legacy A 63 year old Mon farm worker was trampled by a wild elephant in Trat today, April 5, after trying to chase it away . Officials said a strong musth scent was detected at the scene. The incident happened in Bo Phloi subdistrict, Bo Rai district. Rescue workers and local officials went to the scene, where the body was found in a forested area inside Khlong Kaeo Waterfall National Park. Officials quickly removed the body from the forest. The body was later taken to Bo Rai Hospital for relatives to collect for religious rites. The victim’s s

items.title: 燃油价格飙升，航空公司拟削减宋干节后航班
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 近日，受航空燃油价格大幅上涨影响，多家航空公司正考虑在宋干节假期后调整航班规模并减少部分航线，以缓解成本压力。 泰国民航局表示，正密切关注中东局势对航空业的冲击，该因素已推动航油价格显著上升。尽管如此，目前泰国国内航班仍维持正常运营。 在市场调节方面，航空公司通过动态定价及行业协同保障机票供应。提前购票的旅客仍有机会获得相对合理票价，民航局建议乘客通过航空公司官方渠道购票，以确保价格与条款透明。 不过，随着宋干节出行高峰结束及淡季来临，民航局预计航空公司将逐步调整运力，目前已出现部分国际航班频次减少的情况。 宋干节前，为缓解出行压力，民航局联合6家航空公司，在11条国内航线上推出15%至30%的票价优惠，并增加近3万个座位。目前，大多数低价机票已被抢购一空。 与此同时，票价上涨问题仍引发争议。民主党议员指出，部分航线在节日期间票价大幅攀升，例如飞往董里府的单程机票一度高达5000至6000泰铢。他呼吁政府采取结构性措施，降低成本并减轻民众出行负担。 （编译：jinda；审校：wan；来源：bangkokpost） The post 燃油价格飙升，航空公司拟削减宋干节后航班 appeared first on 泰国头条新闻 .

items.title: Northern Thailand declared emergency disaster zone over air quality
items.desc: Northern Thailand declared emergency disaster zone over air quality Legacy Three northern provinces declared emergency disaster zones over hazardous air Chiang Mai, Lamphun, and Phayao have been declared emergency disaster zones as persistent hazardous air quality continues to grip northern Thailand, unlocking emergency funds for provincial governors to deploy relief efforts. The announcement was made on Saturday by the permanent secretary of the Ministry of Interior, Unsit Sampuntharat, as he provided an update on wildfires, haze, and fine-dust pollution affecting 17 provinces across the regi

items.title: 油价+雾霾双重打击！清迈宋干节酒店预订量骤减50%
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 近日，清迈正积极筹备4月7日至19日宋干节系列活动，但受高油价与空气污染影响，旅游市场明显降温，长假期间酒店预订量较往年下降约50%。 今年活动分为传统与娱乐两大板块，包括布施、浴佛等传统仪式。其中，4月13日的佛像巡游将迎请Phra Phuttha Sihing等重要佛像参与。同时，古城护城河周边泼水活动、商圈娱乐活动及周边旅游推广也将同步展开。当地政府已在塔佩门一带搭建舞台与大型喷水装置，并加强安全措施以防游客发生危险。 清迈旅游协会代理主席派讪表示，目前宋干节期间酒店预订率仅约50%–60%，明显低于往年同期的80%–90%。尽管中国、马来西亚、新加坡、韩国、日本等游客带动预订在月初略有回升，但受PM2.5空气污染影响，部分游客转为观望。 与此同时，受油价上涨及供应不确定性影响，泰国国内游客出行意愿下降，自驾游客预订占比仅约2%–5%。 业界呼吁政府尽快稳定燃油供应并加快治理空气污染，以恢复游客信心。分析认为，若宋干节期间降雨缓解雾霾，旅游市场仍有回暖空间，但短期内整体压力依然较大。 （编译：jinda；审校：wan；来源：thairath） The post 油价+雾霾双重打击！清迈宋干节酒店预订量骤减50% appeared first on 泰国头条新闻 .

items.title: 曼谷高温预警：体感温度已达危险级！
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月5日，曼谷环境部门发布预警称，今日曼谷城市热指数已升至“危险”级别，提醒公众注意防暑降温，减少高温时段外出活动。 官方建议，公众需密切关注自身健康状况，如出现异常及时就医，尽量避免在11:00-15:00高温时段外出活动，以降低中暑风险。 对于高风险人群，包括儿童、65岁以上老年人、孕妇、慢性病患者、长期户外工作者、户外运动人群及游客，建议进一步限制户外活动时间，随时观察身体反应，如有不适应立即就医。 根据官方说明，当热指数处于42.0°C至51.9°C之间时，属于“危险”等级；若超过52°C，则进入“极度危险”等级，将对人体健康造成严重威胁。 相关部门呼吁公众合理安排出行时间，避免长时间暴露在高温环境中，并注意补充水分，以应对持续高温天气。 （编译：jinda；审校：wan；来源：pptvhd36） The post 曼谷高温预警：体感温度已达危险级！ appeared first on 泰国头条新闻 .

items.title: 阿努廷“专业内阁”遭质疑，民众信任度低迷
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月5日，泰国国家发展管理学院民调中心NIDA Poll发布主题为“专业部长能否掌控局势？”的最新民调结果。调查结果显示，在政府引入外部专业人士担任内阁部长应对经济与能源危机的背景下，多数民众对其能力缺乏信心。 调查显示，针对内阁三名副总理兼部长（外部专业人士），受访者整体呈现不信任倾向： 副总理兼外交部长西哈萨 · 不信任：30.23% · 不太信任：29.54% · 较为信任：22.82% · 非常信任：16.03% · 不回答/不知道：1.38% 副总理兼商业部长素帕琪 · 不信任：33.89% · 不太信任：28.70% · 较为信任：21.91% · 非常信任：14.35% · 不回答/不知道：1.15% 副总理兼财政部长艾尼迪 · 不信任：40.38% · 不太信任：37.10% · 较为信任：14.12% · 非常信任：7.10% · 不回答/不知道：1.30% 其中，副总理兼财政部长的不信任比例最高，显示民众对其应对经济问题的信心相对较弱。 针对总理兼内政部长阿努廷在应对经济与能源危机方面所作出的努力，超半数受访者表示“不同情”“不太同情”，整体负面评价高于正面。 分析指出，尽管政府尝试引入具备专业背景的官员以增强治理能力，但在当前经济与能源压力持续的情况下，公众信任仍未明显提升，政策成效与社会沟通仍面临考验。 （编译：jinda；审校：wan；来

items.title: Songkhla orders probe after 100,000 litres of diesel found
items.desc: Songkhla orders probe after 100,000 litres of diesel found Legacy Officials in Songkhla found 100,000 litres of diesel stored at a company’s international truck yard in Sadao district yesterday, April 4, after no documents were produced to back earlier claims that the site had a valid fuel storage permit . The case emerged after social media posts alleged that a Malaysian transport operator with a trailer yard for cross-border runs between Thailand and Malaysia had stockpiled a large amount of fuel. Officials in the area inspected the site and alerted administrative officials and police, whose

items.title: Thai government enforces price control on essential goods amid conflict
items.desc: Thai government enforces price control on essential goods amid conflict Legacy Thailand’s government has placed bottled drinking water, seasoning sauces, and plastic pellets under price control to curb potential price hikes due to cost pressures linked to the Middle East conflict. The measure will be enforced for one year, as stated in an announcement from the Central Committee on the Prices of Goods and Services. The announcement, signed by Commerce Minister Suphajee Suthumpun, was published in the Royal Gazette on March 25. Following an assessment of the Middle East conflict, the commi

items.title: 泰北空气危机加剧！3府17县被列入紧急灾害救助范围
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月4日，泰国内政部通报，近期北部地区森林山火导致的烟霾及PM2.5污染持续加剧，清迈、南奔及帕夭3府已正式被划定为紧急灾害救助区域，累计覆盖17个县区。 通报中指出，具体受影响区域如下： 清迈府：包括Hod、Samoeng、Doi Saket、Chiang Dao、Mae Wang、Mae Taeng、Mae Rim 在内共7个县被列为森林山火紧急灾区，涉及辖区内40个镇、338个村及27个村小组 南奔府：因PM2.5污染，Li县被划为紧急灾区，覆盖辖区内8个镇及99个村 帕夭府：包括Mueang Phayao、Chun、Chiang Kham、Chiang Muan、Dok Khamtai、Pong、Mae Chai、Phu Sang、Phu Kamtao在内共9个县因森林山火及PM2.5污染被列为紧急灾区 内政部表示，灾区划定后，各府府尹可依法启用紧急预备金及相关预算，用于灾害应对、污染治理及民众救助与补偿。这一机制将提升地方政府在资源调配与应急响应方面的效率，实现统一指挥与快速处置。 同时，作为最贴近基层的行政负责人，府尹将统筹各部门力量，加快推进救援与减灾工作，缓解民众生活压力，推动问题在地方层面尽快得到实质性解决。 目前，内政部正持续跟踪北部地区山火与空气污染形势，并强化对地方政府的支持，力争尽快恢复正常生产生活秩序。 （编译：jinda；审校：wan

items.title: 以油换油！泰国Bangchak推出“厨房废油换汽车燃油”计划
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月4日，泰国Bangchak集团宣布启动试点项目“Fry to Fly – 2升换1升”，鼓励民众将家庭使用过的废弃食用油用于兑换汽车燃油。根据规则，民众携带2升废弃食用油，即可兑换1升柴油或普通等级汽油，可在参与活动的加油站现场直接加注。 据悉，该项目将于2026年4月6日至30日在曼谷及周边地区的15家Bangchak加油站试行。每人兑换上限为20升废弃食用油（可换约10升汽车燃油）。 据介绍，此举是在原有“Fry to Fly”项目基础上的延伸。此前，该项目主要将回收的废弃食用油用于生产可持续航空燃料。而本次试点则进一步贴近民生，让家庭废油可直接转化为日常出行所需能源。 Bangchak方面表示，该措施亦是企业在当前中东局势紧张、能源价格波动背景下，帮助消费者缓解燃油成本压力的一项具体行动，同时推动资源循环利用与绿色能源发展。 （编译：jinda；审校：wan；来源：thairath） The post 以油换油！泰国Bangchak推出“厨房废油换汽车燃油”计划 appeared first on 泰国头条新闻 .
